sprint_meta.notes: WK47 R1 baseline atlas: single 2048² RGBA texture packing all hero/enemy/worker frames for instanced shader sampling.

wk47_r1_baseline_atlas.what_i_changed: Added game/graphics/unit_atlas.py: UnitAtlasBuilder singleton packing heroes (5 classes), enemies (6 types), and workers (3 types) via HeroSpriteLibrary / EnemySpriteLibrary / WorkerSpriteLibrary clips_for(size=32); row-based shelf layout into 2048×2048 SRCALPHA surface; normalized UV map keyed by (unit_type, class_key, action, frame_index); lookup_uv fallback to first-cell UV; frame_count for debug.

commands_run.command: python -c "import pygame; pygame.init(); pygame.display.set_mode((1,1)); from game.graphics.unit_atlas import UnitAtlasBuilder; a = UnitAtlasBuilder.get(); print(f'Atlas: {a.atlas_surface.get_size()}, Frames: {a.frame_count}'); uv = a.lookup_uv('hero', 'warrior', 'idle', 0); print(f'Warrior idle 0 UV: {uv}'); assert uv[2] > 0; print('PASS')"

recommended_next_actions: Agent 03 (Wave 2): bind atlas Surface to shared Ursina texture and sample UVs from UnitAtlasBuilder in instanced unit draw path.

sprint_meta.notes: Wave 2: blob shadows + projectile instancing (shared outside instance buffer).

what_i_changed.summary: Added shadow_instanced_shader with radial gradient blob shadows on second GeomNode sharing the outside instance buffer (transparent bin 0 before units); negative scale.w skips shadows for projectiles. Packed projectile billboard surface into unit atlas key (vfx, projectile, arrow, 0). InstancedUnitRenderer packs snapshot.vfx_projectiles with negative PROJECTILE_BILLBOARD_SCALE; instanced_unit_shader uses abs(scale) for billboards. UrsinaRenderer skips legacy projectile Entities when instancing is on to avoid double draws. PNG proof under docs/art/.

sprint_meta.notes: v1.6 Round 5: HUD texture pipeline perf + world-space UI as native Ursina entities.

what_i_changed.summary: Added native Ursina health bar quads (background dark + foreground colored) for all 4 entity types: heroes, enemies, peasants, guards. Bars show only when entity is damaged (hp < max_hp), hidden at full health. Left-aligned foreground via center-anchor shift. Green if >50% HP, red otherwise. Bars are billboard, unlit, depth-test disabled.
what_i_changed.pattern: Two child Entity quads (_ks_hp_bg, _ks_hp_fg) parented to billboard ent. Created once, then toggle .enabled and update scale/color/position on subsequent frames.

how_to_test: python main.py --no-llm  (Ursina default: wait for combat, observe damaged entity health bars)

sprint_meta.notes: WK61 sprint: playtest polish features (rubble, enemy panel, chat button, gold overlay, label removal).

what_i_changed.summary: No code changes. Reviewed 5 new visual elements for art cohesion.

details: Rock models (rock_smallA-F.glb) are appropriate: all 6 variants exist under assets/models/environment/. Using the same Kenney low-poly rock props that already appear in terrain scatter keeps visual consistency.
3 rocks per rubble site is a good density: enough to read as debris without cluttering the tile. Comparable to the existing terrain scatter density.

Scale range 0.8-1.5 is LOWER than terrain scatter rocks (ROCK_SCALE_MULTIPLIER=1.68). This is intentional and correct: rubble should read as broken-down chunks, not landscape boulders. The variance (nearly 2x range) gives organic spread.

Color rgb(0.6, 0.55, 0.5) — dusty gray-brown — is appropriate for rubble. It desaturates toward stone/dust tones and reads differently from the brown used for tax collector pouches rgb(0.4, 0.25, 0.1). Good differentiation.
Coordinate system: uses sim_px_to_world_xz() and get_terrain_height() — same pipeline as buildings/units. terrain_y + 0.1 lift prevents Z-fighting with ground plane. Correct.
Deterministic RNG seeded from record_id — follows the codebase convention (no wall-clock or unseeded random). Good.
Scatter offset is +/-30% of half-footprint — rocks cluster near center rather than spilling outside the building footprint. Correct for rubble feel.
1_rubble_rendering.minor_note: The rock_small*.glb list uses CamelCase stems (rock_smallA) while the filesystem also has snake_case variants (rock_small_a.glb). The code only references CamelCase stems. If _environment_model_path fails to resolve CamelCase, it would silently produce invisible rubble. Recommend verifying the model loader handles both naming conventions, or explicitly listing only the stems that match on-disk filenames.

2_enemy_info_panel.verdict: PASS — strong cohesion with existing panels
details: Panel structure mirrors hero_panel.py exactly: same __init__ signature (theme, frame_inner, frame_highlight), same _draw_section_divider method, same header_h=28, same header_rect construction (panel_x+6, panel_y+pad-4, panel_width-12, header_h).

Header background (45,30,30) is a dark warm red, distinct from hero panel's (35,35,45) cool blue-gray. This is a deliberate and correct color shift: enemy = warm/hostile, hero = cool/neutral. Same frame_inner/frame_highlight border treatment.
Enemy name color (220,80,80) — red-tinted title text — matches the hostile theme. Hero panel uses COLOR_WHITE for names. Appropriate differentiation.

HP bar uses uniform red (220,50,50) for good/warn/bad states with a dark red background (60,30,30) and dark border (20,10,10). This reads correctly for enemy health: 'all enemy HP is red.' Hero panel uses green->yellow->red gradient. Correct semantic distinction.

Combat stats section uses consistent text colors: section headers (180,150,150), ATK in warm pinkish (255,200,200), speed in cool blue (200,200,255). These match the muted stat colors used throughout the hero panel.
State and target display at bottom with appropriate muted colors and the same shadow_color=(25,25,35) used everywhere in the HUD.
TextLabel.render and HPBar.render widget usage matches hero_panel exactly — shared widget contract.
Uses _camel_to_display for enemy type name formatting — clean presentation.
Placement at bottom of hero info (after all stats, memory, career) is correct — it is a secondary action, not primary info. The y += 6 gap before it provides visual separation.
Button uses full panel width minus padding (chat_btn_w = panel_width - pad*2), matching the HP bar and XP bar width. Consistent with panel layout.
Height of 26px is compact and proportional to the 28px header height.

Button bg color (60,120,200) / hover (80,150,240) is a distinctive blue. This is appropriate for an interactive element — stands out from the information-display colors without clashing. The blue accent is consistent with the XP bar blue tones (120,170,230) used in the same panel.
Uses frame_inner for the 1px border — same border treatment as headers and section dividers throughout the HUD.
Text is centered both horizontally and vertically within the button rect. White (255,255,255) text on the blue background has strong contrast.
Scroll-aware: uses y - scroll_off for positioning within the scrollable viewport. The _chat_button_rect stores the actual screen position for hit testing.
3_chat_button.minor_note: The button lacks the top-highlight line that headers get (frame_highlight on top edge). Adding a 1px lighter line on the top of the button would match the beveled look of header bars. Not critical — the flat style also works for a button.

details: Gold text color rgb(1.0, 0.8, 0.2) matches the hero gold label color used at line 1388. Consistent gold color across the game.

Text scale=12 matches the hero name label scale=12 at line 1370, and the building gold scale is consistent. The building gold sits at y=0.9, between the HP bar (y=1.5) and the building center. Readable separation.
Billboard=True ensures readability at any camera angle — correct for world-space text.

The G-key toggle is clean: held_keys.get('g', False) checks every frame. When G is released, the elif branch sets gold_ent.enabled = False. This is the same show/hide pattern used for HP bars (enabled = True/False toggle) — no destroy/recreate, so no visual artifacts on release.
Text entity is created once and cached as ent._ks_gold_label — subsequent frames just toggle .enabled and update .text if the stash value changed. No per-frame allocation.
The stash_gold / stored_tax_gold fallback chain is sensible — covers both economic building types.

The label entity is not destroyed, just disabled (label_ent.enabled = False). This is the safest approach: no orphan child entities, no null-pointer crashes from other code that might still reference _ks_label.
The getattr(ent, '_ks_label', None) check means buildings that were never given a label (new buildings created after this change) will skip gracefully.

No visual gap: the HP bar and gold display are positioned at absolute y offsets (y=1.5 for HP, y=0.9 for gold) relative to the building entity, not relative to the label. Removing the label does not shift other elements.

Searched for _ks_label creation — found only this one reference in the renderer. The label entity was likely created in an older version of this function. Since disabled entities remain parented but invisible, there is no artifact risk.
The comment 'WK61-FEAT-001: disabled' is clear and traceable.

wk61_r2_qa_review.overall_verdict: All 5 visual elements pass art cohesion review. No blocking issues. Two minor notes flagged for follow-up but neither requires immediate action.
flagged_for_followup: Rubble: verify _environment_model_path resolves CamelCase 'rock_smallA' stems to the on-disk .glb files (both CamelCase and snake_case variants exist).
Chat button: optional bevel highlight could match header style but the flat button is acceptable.

details: Gold Text rgb(1.0,0.8,0.2) matches hero gold labels; scale=12; y=1.8 above building anchor; depth_test off + always_on_top via _configure_ks_overlay.
Engine polls G each tick via set_tax_gold_overlay_held(input_manager.is_key_pressed('g')).

Workers/guards/tax collector omit _sync_ks_facing_overlay but billboards do not use negative scale_x facing flip — no backwards-label risk on those types.

sprint_meta.notes: WK62 architecture cleanup sprint: Wave 2 UI/render pressure relief.

what_i_changed.summary: Task A: Created game/graphics/visual_specs.py with UnitVisualSpec frozen dataclass and unit_visual_spec() lookup. Specs for hero, enemy, peasant, guard, tax_collector with exact values from ursina_renderer.py. Updated ursina_renderer.py to use HERO_SPEC/ENEMY_SPEC/PEASANT_SPEC/GUARD_SPEC/TAX_COLLECTOR_SPEC for all _ensure_ks_name_label calls (label_y, label_scale). Task B: Created game/graphics/ursina_unit_overlays.py extracting configure_ks_overlay, sync_ks_facing_overlay, ensure_ks_name_label, sync_hp_bar, sync_hero_gold_label, sync_hero_rest_label, sync_hero_overlays_facing, sync_unit_overlays_facing. Updated ursina_renderer.py: original functions now delegate to helpers; all 4 HP bar inline blocks (hero/enemy/peasant/guard) replaced with sync_hp_bar(ent, hp, max_hp, SPEC); hero gold/rest/facing blocks replaced with helper calls.

value_verification.note: visual_specs values cross-checked against ursina_renderer constants: HERO scale=0.9300, ENEMY=0.7500, PEASANT=(0.6300,0.3465), GUARD=(0.7500,1.0500) — all match originals exactly.

follow_ups: instanced_unit_renderer.py and ursina_pick.py still have their own copy of scale constants — future round can point them at visual_specs too.
Building HP bar in _sync_building_worldspace_ui uses different constants (not unit overlays); separate extraction if needed.
Per-class hero spec overrides (e.g., wizard label colour) can be added to unit_visual_spec() when needed.

sprint_meta.notes: WK66 behavior-preserving render-boundary refactor. Agent 09 consult-only: Gate 3 broad visual-cohesion verdict across all pygame + Ursina render paths. No production code.

wk66_g3_final.role: consult - Gate 3 broad visual-cohesion verdict (no code; behavior-preserving render-boundary refactor)

what_i_changed.summary: Reviewed BASELINE (docs/screenshots/wk66_baseline) vs AFTER (wk66_after_w2 = renderer/DTO wave; wk66_after_w3 = L10 terrain/fog move, pygame-only) for every render path. Method: sha256 byte-compare on deterministic static pygame scenes; PIL bbox/changed-pixel quantification on the building-catalog closeups (to distinguish regression from capture jitter); direct visual composition review on dynamic pygame + all 3 Ursina scenes. No production code touched.

wk66_g3_final.verdict_overall: PASS - every render path is identical or compositionally cohesive. No real (non-jitter) visual difference found anywhere. Bounty labels, building labels/HP bars, unit health bars, fog edges all unchanged.

per_path_verdicts.pyg_building_catalog_overview: IDENTICAL - byte-identical (sha256) baseline vs w2 vs w3; visual confirm: full building grid + paths + fog + central units match.
per_path_verdicts.pyg_building_catalog_closeups(34): COHESIVE - these are NOT static catalog shots; each is a live base scene with a battle in progress (moving heroes/goblins, debris VFX). sha256 differs AND w2-vs-w3 also differs (both post-change) => capture-nondeterministic, not regression. PIL diffs are localized to unit/combat bboxes (blacksmith changed 0.9-2.3pct lower-left only; palace 0.37pct corner). Buildings with no animated overlay are byte-identical across all 3 (castle, ranger/rogue/warrior guild). Visual confirm (inn, largest diff 16pct): same building grid + same labels (RANGERS/ROGUES/WIZARDS/MARKET/SMITH/AGRELA/DAUROS/FERVUS/GNOMES/ELVES/LIBRARY/GARDENS) + same HP bars + same fog edge + same debris VFX; only unit positions/anim frames differ.
per_path_verdicts.pyg_enemy_catalog(7): IDENTICAL - all 7 closeups + overview byte-identical; visual confirm of overview matches.
per_path_verdicts.pyg_worker_catalog(3): IDENTICAL - peasant/tax_collector closeups + overview byte-identical; HP bars + CASTLE label + worker sprites render identically.
per_path_verdicts.pyg_bounty_fog(2): IDENTICAL - closeup + overview byte-identical baseline vs w3 (post bounty-label-cache relocation). Visual confirm: $100 reward (white), gold flag sprite, R:0 responder (white), High tier (green), black fog square edges all pixel-identical. The relocated label cache produced ZERO visual change.
per_path_verdicts.pyg_building_debris(2): IDENTICAL - closeup + overview byte-identical; castle sprite, blue roof, foundation platform, hero, debris all unchanged.
per_path_verdicts.pyg_world_variation(2): IDENTICAL - closeup + overview byte-identical baseline vs w3 (the L10 terrain/fog-move wave). Terrain field, tree clumps, fog, full HUD/minimap/command-bar all unchanged. L10 terrain/fog refactor is visually invisible.

Visual confirm Z1: same map shape, building positions, cross-roads, fog edges, HUD, perf panel, minimap, command bar; only moving-unit positions vary.
per_path_verdicts.pyg_ranged_projectiles(2): COHESIVE - closeup byte-identical; overview differs (live combat scene). Visual confirm: same Gareth hero label, same enemy+HP bar, same ranger+green HP bar, same HOUSE+HP bar, same CASTLE+hero+platform, same fog/trees; only anim frame/projectile-in-flight differ. Projectile/attack VFX + unit HP bars styled/placed identically.
per_path_verdicts.ursina_base: COHESIVE - composition identical: same 3D village island, buildings, units, trees, fog-disabled banner (32 POIs revealed), HUD, minimap, command bar. Only perf-overlay digits jitter (ignored per PM).
per_path_verdicts.ursina_tax_overlay(hold_g): COHESIVE - composition identical: same close-up village row, same buildings, same red-cloaked hero, same teal markers, Gold:2443 HUD, minimap, command bar. No tax-overlay tint/label artifact. Only perf digits jitter.
per_path_verdicts.ursina_fog: COHESIVE - composition identical to ursina_base; island/village/units/HUD all match. Only perf digits jitter.

high_risk_paths_confirmed_unchanged: Bounty labels (reward $ / R: responder / tier) - cache moved off sim object - byte-identical, pixel-perfect.
Building labels + HP bars + tax overlays - unchanged (pygame catalogs byte/visually identical; Ursina tax overlay cohesive).
Unit health bars - unchanged (worker_catalog byte-identical; debris/base/projectile bars visually identical; Ursina hp bars cohesive).

wk66_g3_final.real_differences_found: NONE. Every sha256 DIFF traces to a dynamic/animated scene (live combat or moving units) and is confirmed by PIL bbox localization + visual composition review to be capture jitter, not a render-boundary regression.

known_gap.issue: No scripted Ursina melee-combat / attack one-shot capture scenario. Only wk61_hold_g_tax_overlay is registered for Ursina. The attack/hurt one-shot on the Ursina path was verified by a headless unit test, not a capture.
known_gap.recommendation: ADD a registered Ursina combat-capture scenario as a FOLLOW-UP (not a Gate-3 blocker). Rationale: (1) Ursina is the primary renderer and is the path with the least visual capture coverage; (2) the DTO/render-boundary change specifically affects how attack/hurt anim state reaches the renderer, so a one-shot capture would give a visual (not just unit-test) regression guard for future refactors; (3) the pygame ranged_projectiles scenario already proves the pattern is feasible. Suggest a deterministic scripted scenario that spawns a hero+enemy adjacent, forces an attack tick, and captures the strike + hurt frame. Owner: Agent 12 (capture harness) + Agent 06 (combat state). Gate 3 can pass WITHOUT it because the one-shot is already unit-test-covered.

how_to_test: Open PNG pairs in docs/screenshots/wk66_baseline vs wk66_after_w2 / wk66_after_w3
Deterministic static scenes: byte-compare (PowerShell Get-FileHash) on bounty_fog/building_debris/world_variation/enemy_catalog/worker_catalog/building_catalog_overview => identical
Dynamic scenes (base_overview, building_catalog closeups, ranged_projectiles): judge composition, not pixels

byte_compare.note: byte-DIFF on building_catalog closeups is NOT a regression - w2 and w3 (both post-change) also differ from each other => animation/combat jitter.

follow_ups: FOLLOW-UP: register a deterministic Ursina melee-combat/attack one-shot capture scenario (Agent 12 + Agent 06) - see known_gap.recommendation.

Catalog scene naming is misleading: pyg_building_catalog *_closeup shots are live battle scenes, not static catalog frames - consider a static/paused capture variant so they can be byte-judged like the other catalogs.

sprint_meta.notes: WK68 Wave R2 (Agent 09): Ursina + pygame-remainder renderers migrated onto frozen render DTOs; entity tracking keyed on stable entity_id not id(obj). Behavior-preserving (WK67 digest unchanged). One lair-misdetection regression found-and-fixed via screenshot review.

wk68_r2_dto_migration.role: WAVE R2 - migrate Ursina + pygame-remainder renderers onto frozen render DTOs; key entity tracking on stable entity_id (string) not id(obj). Behavior-preserving.

game/graphics/pygame_renderer.py: guards->guard_dtos / peasants->peasant_dtos / tax_collector->tax_collector_dto (registry render_* fed DTOs; getattr(x,render_state,x) returns the DTO, same as the already-migrated hero/enemy draws)
building_list_panel.render(snapshot.buildings)->building_dtos (availability reads building_type+is_constructed; DTO lowercase str == BuildingType str-enum)

update_ui_metrics(heroes/enemies/buildings) LEFT on live entities ON PURPOSE - sim-state mutation keyed on live-object identity (hero.target[bounty_ref] is bounty) + estimate_risk(enemies)/is_valid(buildings); a frozen DTO cannot satisfy it. Flagged for relocation before R3.
game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py: _sync_snapshot_heroes/enemies/peasants/guards/tax_collector: iterate the matching *_dtos tuple; key self._entities on dto.entity_id (not id) across create/cull/re-enable/destroy; facing via _facing_from_dto; base clip via _base_clip_from_dto; pass key=entity_id to get_or_create_entity.

_sync_snapshot_buildings: iterate building_dtos; entity_id keying across dead/underground/POI-undiscovered/frustum-cull/lair-fog branches; center_x/center_y for placement+cull+fog; is_lair from dto.is_lair|has_stash_gold; poi_type for cave/mine tint; key= on all 3 building get_or_create_* variants.
NEW _base_clip_from_dto(dto): byte-for-byte port of the 5 ursina_units_anim base-clip helpers (reads dto.kind/state_name/is_inside_building/is_alive).
NEW _facing_from_dto(dto): port of _unit_facing_direction; movement scratch moved off the live entity into self._unit_facing_state keyed by entity_id; combat branch uses dto.target_x.
_compute_anim_frame: reads dto.anim_trigger/anim_trigger_seq (fallback to legacy entity attrs); base_clip_fn optional (None -> _base_clip_from_dto).
building_tax_overlay_snapshot: DTO path via has_tax_overlay + stored_tax_gold (legacy live method path kept as fallback).
hero is_resting forced False (reproduces legacy enum!=str no-op; latent dead Zzz label - flagged to Agent 08).
REMAINING live read: ursina:1821 bounty fallback (only when bounty_dtos is None - never in prod). Flagged for R3.
game/graphics/ursina_entity_render_colla: get_or_create_entity / get_or_create_3d_building_entity / get_or_create_prefab_building_entity: added key=None param; obj_id = id(sim_obj) if key is None else key. Callers pass key=dto.entity_id. Projectiles still use id().
game/graphics/ursina_prefabs.py (cross-l: NEW _building_is_lair(building): DTO-safe lair check. BuildingDTO carries a stash_gold int field on EVERY building, so legacy hasattr(b,stash_gold) was True for ALL DTOs -> mis-detected castle/guilds as lairs (castle rendered as gray lair mesh). New check uses has_stash_gold field when present else live hasattr; is_lair authoritative. Applied in _is_3d_mesh_building/_mesh_kind_for_building/_resolve_prefab_path. Verified 0 render-path mismatches across all 43 buildings vs live.
game/sim/render_dto.py (cross-lane, ADDI: UnitDTO: + target_x: float|None (Ursina facing combat-target) + state: str (alias of state_name; pygame WorkerRenderer reads entity.state for the tax Collecting... label).
BuildingDTO: + has_tax_overlay: bool (== has_tax_stash_data; hold-G gold overlay) + grid_x/grid_y/size (terrain scatter-exclusion + grid-debug; == _building_occupied_tiles reads).
Builders populate all of the above exactly as the renderers read the live entity.

wk68_r2_dto_migration.entity_id_keying_consistency: self._entities keyed by dto.entity_id (string) for all 5 unit kinds + buildings; active_ids collects the SAME entity_id strings; _destroy_removed_entities does set(self._entities.keys()) - active_ids (works with string keys); cull/re-enable/dead/underground/POI branches all use entity_id; get_or_create_*(key=entity_id) makes creation use the same key. _unit_anim_state and _unit_facing_state keyed by the same entity_id and pruned in _destroy_removed_entities. Projectiles stay id()-keyed (int, out of scope) - mixed int/string keys never collide in the set diff. Verified: entity_ids are stable strings, unique within a frame, persistent across 30 ticks (43/43 buildings).
wk68_r2_dto_migration.dto_fields_added: 6 additive fields (UnitDTO: target_x, state; BuildingDTO: has_tax_overlay, grid_x, grid_y, size). All defaults reproduce the live read.

screenshots.pygame_after: docs/screenshots/wk68_r2_pygame_after (base_overview) + wk68_r2_pygame_panels_after (ui_panels, 7 PNG incl. tax_collector + building)

per_kind_visual_verdict.enemies: PASS. Ursina: ONE Goblin with label + HP bar - no duplicate. Fog-gated visibility unchanged.
per_kind_visual_verdict.peasants: PASS. pygame: same distribution, no ghost/missing.
per_kind_visual_verdict.guards: PASS. pygame: guard units with HP bars.
per_kind_visual_verdict.tax_collector: PASS + critical proof: pygame ui_panels_tax_collector shows the collector diamond with the Collecting... state label intact (validates the DTO state alias).
per_kind_visual_verdict.buildings: PASS after fixing the lair-misdetection regression. Castle renders as the brick castle prefab; guilds/houses varied prefabs; Ursina tax-overlay AFTER pixel-matches BEFORE. Labels/HP bars correct.
per_kind_visual_verdict.bounties: PASS. Ursina flags already on bounty_dtos; pygame bounty draw now on bounty_dtos.
per_kind_visual_verdict.alignment_layering: PASS. pygame HUD/panel positions identical before/after; Ursina world placement identical (center_x/center_y == live x/y).

wk68_r2_dto_migration.regression_found_and_fixed: YES - one. Initial building migration rendered EVERY building (incl. castle/guilds) as a gray LAIR mesh because BuildingDTO carries a stash_gold int field, making legacy hasattr(b,stash_gold) True for all DTOs. Fixed via _building_is_lair (use has_stash_gold field; is_lair authoritative). Caught by before/after screenshot review + a prefab-resolution parity probe (0/43 mismatches after fix). Re-captured Ursina AFTER matches BEFORE.
follow_ups_for_PM_and_agent_03: R3 BLOCKER: pygame bounty_system.update_ui_metrics(heroes/enemies/buildings) still reads the live snapshot tuples (pygame_renderer.py:157-159,256-258). It is a sim-state mutation matching heroes by live-object identity + mutating live bounties - it cannot be DTO-fed in the renderer. Relocate it to the sim tick before R3 deletes the live tuples.
R3 minor: ursina_renderer.py:1821 defensive bounties = getattr(snapshot, bounties, ()) fallback (WK66) only reached when bounty_dtos is None (never in prod). Remove with the live-tuple deletion.

Agent 08 (UX): the Ursina hero RESTING Zzz indicator was a latent no-op (legacy compared a plain HeroState Enum to the string RESTING -> always False). Preserved the no-op (is_resting=False) for pixel parity; reviving it is a UX fix in their lane.
CAP-001 confirmed: run_ursina_capture_once has no base_overview scenario (known: ursina_melee_combat, wk61_hold_g_tax_overlay). Used both for Ursina coverage.
wk68_r2_dto_migration.cross_lane_edits_note: Core migration stayed in ursina_renderer.py + pygame_renderer.py. Three minimal cross-lane touches, all backward-compatible: (1) render_dto.py ADDITIVE fields only (sanctioned escape hatch); (2) ursina_entity_render_collab.py additive key= param (the entity-map keying lives there; PM prompt directed refactoring get_or_create_entity to take the DTO); (3) ursina_prefabs.py _building_is_lair correctness fix without which every building renders as a lair. All preserve live-entity behavior byte-for-byte.
